title: Explicit heat grid step equals center plus scaled second difference

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: For a real-valued grid state u, the shifted three-point heat update equals the center value plus r times the second difference u(i) - 2u(i+1) + u(i+2).
assumptions: r is a real diffusion number
u is a real-valued grid state on natural-number indices
i is a natural-number grid index
informal.expected_conclusion: The grid-window update unfolds to center plus scaled second difference.

declarations.name: heatStepGrid
declarations.statement: def heatStepGrid (r : Real) (u : Nat -> Real) (i : Nat) : Real := heatStep r (u i) (u (i + 1)) (u (i + 2))

declarations.name: heatStepGrid_eq_center_plus_second_difference
declarations.statement: theorem heatStepGrid_eq_center_plus_second_difference (r : Real) (u : Nat -> Real) (i : Nat) : heatStepGrid r u i = u (i + 1) + r * (u i - 2 * u (i + 1) + u (i + 2))

tasks.input: Translate this explicit heat-equation finite-difference theorem into Lean 4: For a real-valued grid state u, the shifted three-point heat update equals the center value plus r times the second difference u(i) - 2u(i+1) + u(i+2).
tasks.target: theorem heatStepGrid_eq_center_plus_second_difference (r : Real) (u : Nat -> Real) (i : Nat) : heatStepGrid r u i = u (i + 1) + r * (u i - 2 * u (i + 1) + u (i + 2))

tasks.input: Given the heat-equation scaffold definitions and theorem statement, complete the Lean 4 proof.
tasks.target: A proof by unfolding heatStepGrid, heatStep, and the weight definitions, then using ring.
